publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-03-07 23:22:16 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Architectural plans and sketches, professional correspondence, client and project files, organizational files, and committee reports of Charles N. Lowrie (1895-1939), an American landscape architect and designer known for his state and public parks and his involvement in the founding of the American Society of Landscape Architects.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Charles Nassau Lowrie (April 8, 1869 – September 18, 1939) was an American landscape architect and designer born in Warriors Mark, Pennsylvania. He graduated from Yale University's Sheffield Scientific School as a Civil Engineer in 1891 and established an architectural practice in New York City in 1896. Some of his earliest works include plans for the Edgar Allen Poe Park, Claremont Park, and the South Bronx Park in New York City and the proposed Fire Island State Park. In 1899, Lowrie was among a group of eleven landscape architects who founded the American Society of Landscape Architects. He served as Treasurer of the society from 1899 to 1909, as Vice President from 1909-1910, and as President of the society from 1910 to 1912. He was also the founder and first president of the New York Chapter of the Society. Throughout his career, Lowrie served on numerous arts and public works committees and commissions, including the ASLA committee on Parks, the City Committee on Plan and Survey, the Fine Arts Federation of New York Committee on West Side Improvement, the Mayor's West Side Improvement Architects' Committee, and the Park Association of New York City. He also held membership in the Century Association and the Yale Engineering Association. Additionally, in 1938, under the provision of the new Charter of New York City, Lowrie was appointed the first landscape architect to sit as a member of the Arts Commission of the City of New York.

Lowrie's professional works included landscaping and general architectural plans for county park, state park, and city planning commissions. He designed and executed the Roosevelt Memorial Park at Oyster Bay, Long Island, and made preliminary plans for Valley Stream Park, Hempstead Park, Hanse Park, and Jones Beach for the Long Island State Park Commission. He served for 30 years as landscape architect for the Hudson County Parks Commission of New Jersey, designing and executing several parks for the county's park system, including West Side Park (now Lincoln Park) and Pershing Field in Jersey City, Bayonne Park (now the Stephen R. Gregg Park) in Bayonne, Columbus Park in Hoboken, and West Hudson Park in Harrison. During World War I, Lowrie served as town planner for the United States Housing Corporation on its industrial housing projects in New Brunswick (NJ), New London (CT), and Alton (Ill). He was also a member of the Columbus (OH) Plan Commission from 1907 to 1908.

Lowrie worked in several capacities with schools and universities in the creation and execution of campus improvement and extension initiatives and landscaping plans. Under the New Jersey State Board of Education, he prepared plans for the New Jersey School for the Deaf and the State Normal Schools at Glassboro and Montclair. He also conducted work on the campuses of Cornell University, the Connecticut Agricultural College (now the Ratcliffe-Hicks School of Agriculture at the University of Connecticut), the New Britain Normal School (now Central Connecticut State University), and the East Stroudsburg State Normal School in Pennsylvania. He also prepared plans for the Peekskill Military Academy, the Mount Harmon School for Boys, and the Princeton Seminary.

Lowrie also drafted and executed landscaping plans and general plans for numerous private residences, estates, and partitions of privately-owned land, including for notable New York and New Jersey politicians and businessmen such as Fredrick Frelinghuysen, Jr., Garret Hobart, Jr., Louis F. Rothschild, and George W. and Jacob Gould Schurman. The estate of George W. Schurman would go on to be purchased by the Bouvier family and named Lasata, later becoming the beloved summer home of former First Lady Jackie Kennedy Onassis. In 1906, Lowrie was hired by William M. and Gertrude Divine Ritter to prepare a planting plan and designs for a teahouse for their home in Columbus, Ohio. In 1917, Lowrie was again hired by the Ritters to prepare a landscape plan for the grounds of their new home in Manchester, Vermont. Known as Yester House, the Ritter's Vermont property is now home to the Southern Vermont Arts Center.
Lowrie died in New York in 1939 while on the staff of the New York World's Fair and was buried in Lawrenceville, New Jersey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The Charles N. Lowrie papers (1892-1939) documents the architectural practice of Charles N. Lowrie (1869-1939), American landscape architect and founding member of the American Society of Landscape Architects. The collection contains architectural plans for residential, educational, recreational, and public works projects; professional correspondence; transcripts of speeches and radio talks; client and project files; organizational files; and committee reports. Organizational and client files include materials from American Society of Landscape Architects, the Mayor's Committee on West Side Improvement, the Long Island State Park Commission, the Hudson County Park System, the 1939 World's Fair, Cornell University, and parks and schools in New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut. The collection also includes a small amount of landscape photography of completed projects, including Bayonne Park and Columbus Park, as well as photocopies of architectural plans. The collection includes numerous plans, drafts, and blueprints from the United States Housing Corporation's industrial housing projects in New Brunswick (NJ), New London (CT), Alton (IL), and Columbus (OH). The collection does not include architectural plans or drawings related to the 1939 New York World's Fair.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The papers, organizational records, correspondence, photography, and a number of architectural plans in this collection were donated to Cornell University by Smith College in 2004 after the papers were discovered in the Alice Pecknagel Ireys papers. Alice Ireys was a landscape architect and a student of Lowrie who inherited a number of his clients after his death in 1939. The bulk of the architectural plans in this collection were donated to Cornell University by the Hudson County Parks System in 2016.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Maps rolled in tubes may be fragile, care is reccomended when handling. Maps wrapped in paper tubes are very fragile and require care when being handled. Many maps are hand-drawn originals and can stain hands and fingers, be aware of possible transfer when moving between materials.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charles N. Lowrie Papers, #6611. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: The following records contain materials regarding the United States Housing Corporation, for which Lowire served as Town Planner in Alton, Il; New Brunswick, NJ; and New London, CT:

Architectural and Engineering Plans; Records of the Town Planning Division; Records of USHS Divisions and Related Units; United States Housing Corporation, Record Group 3; National Archives, Washington, D.C.
Architectural and Engineering Plans; Cartographic Records (General); United States Housing Corporation, Record Group 3; National Archives, Washington, D.C.
The following collection contains additional correspondence between Charles L. Beach and Charles N. Lowrie regarding Lowrie's work with the Connecticut Agricultural College:
#1988-0014 University of Connecticut, President's Office Records [Charles L. Beach 1908-1928]. Archives and Special Collections, University of Connecticut Library.

controlaccess.subject: Parks -- New York (State) -- Planning.
Parks -- New Jersey -- Planning.
Schools -- New York (State)
Landscape architects.
Landscape architects

City planning
Housing--Connecticut.
Housing--New Jersey.
controlaccess.corpname: New York (N.Y.). Committee on West Side Improvement
New York World's Fair (Location of meeting: New York, N.Y.). Date of meeting or treaty signing: (1939-1940 :.)

did.unittitle: "Articles of and by Charles N. Lowrie"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes index of contents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence signed by Helen Keller from the American Foundation for the Blind.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and financial records relating to Lowrie's renting of an office in New York Cit for his architectural practice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from former colleagues and clients reccomending Lowrie for projects and committees.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcripts of radio talks on landscape design given by Lworie, a talk given by Lworie at the annual meeting of the American Society of Civil Engineers, and a summary of a talk given before the New York chapter of the American Society of Landscape Architects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlets, articles, and bulletins published by the Regional Plan Association, Inc. of New York City. Also includes a copy of "The Training of a Landscape Architect" by F. L. Olmstead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preliminary sketches, plans, blueprints, and pamphlets of work done by Lowrie. Includes blueprint of sketch for Lawrenceville School, Lawrenceville, NJ.

did.unittitle: Series II. Organization and Committee Files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Meeting minutes, committee reports, pamphlets, and correspondence from professional organizaions and public works committees Lowrie was a part of.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, materials created by the Central Park Association, the Central Park Protection Committee, and the Architectural League of New York, and a pamphlet from the Metropolitan Conference of City and State Park Authorities, February 1926.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes constitutions and by-laws, membership lists, and committees.

did.unittitle: ASLA Committee on Parks, New York Chapter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes several talks by Gilmore D. Clark on the planning and protection of state and city parks and statements written by ASLA to be published in newspapers.

did.unittitle: City Committee on Plan and Survey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding Lowrie's appointment to the sub-committee on Parks and Recreational Facilities of the City Committee on Plan and Survey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and committee reports from the Fine Arts Federation of New York's Committee on West Side Improvement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and financial records regarding Lowrie's involvement in the Commission. For parks designed by Lowrie as part of the Commission, see Long Island State Park Commission folder in Clients and Project Files.

did.unittitle: Mayor's West Side Improvement Architects' Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a recommendation from Henry W. Sackett to Governor Alfred Smith regarding Lowire's work as a landscape architect for Cornell University.

did.unittitle: Park Association of New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlet of the seventh annual report of the Park Association of New York City and a clipping detailing the proposed restoration of Bryant Park.

did.unittitle: Series III. Client and Project Files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Corespondence, committee reports, and cost estimates for various proposed projects. Includes correspondence from Garret A. Hobart, Jr., the Stony Brook Association, the Park Avenue Association, W.M Lawson, Lewis H. Bronson, and F.H. Bennett regarding the Wheatsworth Mill in Hamburg, NJ.

did.unittitle: American Legion Monument, Glens Falls, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence to T. Arthur Ball regarding treatment of a terrace garden. Includes descriptions of preliminary sketches by Lowrie. These preliminary sketches are not [included] in the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Lowrie and the Bayonne Board of Education, cost estimates for landsape work for Bayonne Junior High School, and a Bayonne Board of Education resolution apponting Lowrie as Landscape Architect for Bayonne Junior High School. Includes a pamphlet, "The Method of Practice and Charges" of the New York Chapter of ASLA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contracts between the Boulevard Gardens Housing Corporation and Charles Lowrie for his work on the Boulevard Gardens Housing Project. Includes photocopies of originals plans drawn by Lowrie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding the Columbus Country Club and Franklin Park.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Charles L. Beach, President of the Connecticut Agricultural College regarding the securing of the advice of a landscape architect for the layout of the campus. Also includes correspondence regarding the proposed establishment of the Ratcliff Hicks School of Agriculture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a a pamphlet, "A Report on the Campus and Grounds of Cornell University," authored by Lowrie and which includes maps of the campus of Cornell Univeristy in 1904.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a report on and cost estimates for the Edison Memorial Park at Menlo Park, New Jersey, authored by Lowire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketches of planting plans for street islands at Stanbery Place, Bexley, Ohio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding work on North Hudson Park and property around Bayonne Park, cost estimates for work in 1923-1924, and a memorandum on the acquisition and development of a park system for Hudson County. Includes a copy of a bulletin released by the Regional Plan Association regarding park progress in the New York-New Jersey region.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports on Specifications for Landscape Work for A. Harry Moore School and Henry Snyder Junior High School in Jersey City, New Jersey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, cost estimates, and plans for Hanse Park, Hempstead Park, Jones Beach State Park, and Valley Stream Pond Park as part of the Long Island State Park Commission

did.unittitle: Mount St. Dominic Academy, Caldwell, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Mount St. Dominic Academy, an all-girls Catholic high school in Caldwell, NJ.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills for services rendered by Lowrie to Merck & Company. Includes a photograph of the exterior of the Merck & Co. headquarters.

did.unittitle: New Jersey School for the Deaf
New York Association for the Blind

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report by Lowire on property proposed by the New York Association for the Blind for a summer camp site.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding the extension of the New York Capital building grounds in Albany, New York. Includes map of Albany and Rensselaer and a postcard of the State Capitol building.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Agreement between Charles N. Lowrie and New York World's Fair 1939 Incorporated, Landscape - Section 2.

did.unittitle: Oyster Bay Grade and High School, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report and cost estimates for the development of the Grade and High School grounds and an advertisement accepting propoals for landscape inprovement of the Grade and High School grounds. Includes photocopies of original plans drawn by Lowrie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alternate sketches for the garden of Edmund W. Poor, Bayshore, Long Island, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A survey and report on Riverside Park, Manhattan, NY.

did.unittitle: Roosevelt Memorial Park, Oyster Bay, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several plans and contacts for work performed for the Roosevelt Memorial Park, Oyster Bay. Includes plans regarding filling and grading, dredging, the construction of a timber bulkhead and a seawall, and for the laying of electric and telephone lines.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memos, reports on the commission and development of Sandy Hook State Park.

did.unittitle: Schurman, G.W. and Schurman, Jacob Gould

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding planned landscape work on the property of Jacob Gould Schurman.

did.unittitle: State Normal School, East Stroundsburg, PA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Beatrix Farrand, consulting landscape architect at Yale University from 1923-1945, regarding work being considered for the Yale athletic fields.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Landscape photography of Bayonne Park (now the Stephen R. Gregg Park) in Bayonne, Hudson County, New Jersey. Includes photography by Alfred Tennyson Beals and Samuel H. Gottscho.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prints and negatives of Columbus Park in Hoboken, Hudson County, New Jersey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of plans for United States Housing Corporation projects in Alton, Ill; New Brunswick, NJ; and New London, CT. Includes two original photos taken during and after completion of construction on the New London site.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of plans for W.M. Ritter, Crown Point Reservation, NJ, and the New Jersey Naval Training and Industrial School for Colored Youth, Bordentown, NJ.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs North Hudson Park and Lincoln Park, Hudson County, NJ; the E.F. Hutton esate, "Hillwood," Brookville, NY; a photo of the New York City Botanical Gardens, and untitled photography by Sameul H. Gottscho.

did.unittitle: Roosevelt Memorial Park, Oyster Bay, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three particle boards of landscape photography of Roosevelt Memorial Park in Oyster Bay, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for projects on which Lowire was employed as landscape architect.

did.unittitle: Subseries Va. Land and Private Residences

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the estates of G.A. Beaton, W.E. Becker, L.H. Bronson, J.J. Chew, R. Colgate, M.J. Condon, R.N. Depew, C.B. Eddy, H. Fisk, and F. Frelinghuysen.

did.unittitle: Denman, W.F.l Hamill, J.L. and Hamill, S.M.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Date only applies to the Denman plans. Hamill plans are undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Date only applies to the Hathaway plans. Howe plans are undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Hobart Manor, known alternatively as Halendon Hall and Ailsa Farms, for Garret A. Hobart, Jr. and Jennie Tuttle Hobart, the family of 24th Vice President of the United States Garret Hobart. Includes plans from W.L. Whitmore and J.W. Higgins, Civil Engineers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the estates of E.C. Klepstein, G.W. Lattimer, S.J. Leonard, T. Lindenerg, C. Lindenberg, O.E. Lohrke, M. Mead and S.F. Mead. Also includes plan for the Mead Cemetery, Lake Waccabuc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the estates of C.J.S. Miller, F.A. Miller, F.A. Moody, the Morris & Winthrop estates, G. Merck, and E.B. Osborne.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the estates of G.A. Peabody, T.S. Ralston, G.W. Schurman, G.M. Sidenburg, E. Smith, H.A. Smith, H.K. Smith, and J.E. Springarn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the estates of William M. and Gertrude D. Ritter in Columbus, Ohio, and Manchester, Vermont. Inlcudes plans for a tea house on the Columbus property.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the Bayshore, NY estate of Simon F. Rothschild, American merchant and philanthropist.

did.unittitle: Sibley, E.N.; VanderBeek, F.I. - Warden, W.G.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the estates of E.N. Sibley, F.I. VanderBeek, H.W. Walker, and W.G. Warden.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the estates of J.A. Weeks, C.C. Whitcomb, R.T. Wolfe, W.H. Woolverton, J.T. Williams, and C. Witbeck.

did.unittitle: Subseries Vb. City Planning, Parks, Schools

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Pope Manufacturing, Hartford (1895); Beaver Hills Co, New Haven (1910); and the New Britain Normal School (1922-1923).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Groton (1918); New London industrial housing project (1918-1919); and Greenwich Township (1920).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General and topographic maps of the area, street and site plans, grading and planting plans, sewer and water supply plans, street profiles, property maps of the Alton industrial housing development, a project of the United States Housing Corporation for which Lowrie served as town planner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes plans for Preston Pond, Winchester (1895); the Home for Aged Women, Worcester (1896); Mount Hermon School (1907); Leister Commons, Worcester; and Worcester Polytechnic Institute.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Llewellyn Park, Orange (1911); Big Brother Farm, Stockton (1912); Newark Bay (1914); and Bayonne Park (1917).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General and topographic maps of the area, street and site plans, grading and planting plans, sewer and water supply plans, street profiles, property maps of the New Brunswick industrial housing development, a project of the United States Housing Corporation for which Lowrie served as town planner. Predominantly contains blueprints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General and topographic maps of the area, street and site plans, grading and planting plans, sewer and water supply plans, street profiles, property maps of the New Brunswick industrial housing development, a project of the United States Housing Corporation for which Lowrie served as town planner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General and topographic maps of the area, street and site plans, grading and planting plans, sewer and water supply plans, street profiles, property maps of the New Brunswick industrial housing development, a project of the United States Housing Corporation for which Lowrie served as town planner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General and topographic maps of the area, street and site plans, grading and planting plans, sewer and water supply plans, street profiles, property maps of the New Brunswick industrial housing development, a project of the United States Housing Corporation for which Lowrie served as town planner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plan for Riverside Park as part of the New Brunswick industrial housing project.

did.unittitle: New Jersey - New Brunswick - Buccleuch Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the State Normal Schools of Montclair and Glassboro. Predominantly contains plans for the State Normal School at Montclair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the State Normal Schools of Montclair and Glassboro.

did.unittitle: New Jersey School for the Deaf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the New Jersey School for the Deaf, Trenton, NJ.

did.unittitle: New York City, New York; Alton, Ill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Edgar Allen Poe Park and Ft. Washington Park, NYC. Includes a plan for Alton industrial housing project, Illinois.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes plans for Fire Island State Park and Stony Brook State Park.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Predominantly contains plans for Ellis Hospital (1906) and Schenectady Realty Co. (1911); includes plans for Garrison, NY (1899) and Amenia, NY (1922).

did.unittitle: New York - Schenectady - Mohawk Golf Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Peekskill Military Academy (1906); Lake Waccabuc (1906, 1910); Briarcliff Lodge (1910); and Falls Creek and Cascadillia Glen gorges in Ithaca (1915).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Massilon Cemetery, Massilon; Stanbery Place, Bexley, OH; water purification plans for the Columbus Plan Commision and plans for Commonwealth Park, Columbus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the Columbus Plan Commission.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for streets in Philadephia (1898), German Reform Cemetery, Easton (1901), and Blair Memorial Hospital, Huntingdon (1911).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for Franklin (1898); and The River Common, Wilkes-Barre (1096).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the Pennsylvania Masonic Home, a retirement community in Elizabethtown, Lancaster County, PA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for the Pennsylvania Masonic Home, a retirement community in Elizabethtown, Lancaster County, PA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes plans for Columbus, OH, and New York and New Jersey parks. Also includes plans for the estate of G.W. Schurman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mock-up of entrance to Massilon Cemetery, Massilon, OH; mock-up of pillars for Resivoir Park, Jersey City, NJ.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes plans for East Hampton, Long Island; North Hudson, NJ; Edison Memorial Park, NJ; Lawrenceville School, NJ, and the estate of T. Arthur Ball.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans and drawings that do not contain labels identifiying project, date, client, or primary architect.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans and drawings that are verifiably not Lowrie's work or part of projects that Lowrie was connected with. Inlcudes sketches of St. Bernard's Cemetery created by landscape architect Daniel W. Langton, fellow founding member of ASLA.
